question.text: Under SEBI (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993, a debenture trustee is required to appoint a nominee director on the Board of the issuer company in which of the following events?

answer.text: Any single default in payment of interest to debenture holders
Two consecutive defaults in payment of interest to debenture holders
Three consecutive defaults in payment of interest to debenture holders
Only when the issuer company defaults on principal redemption

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 15(e) states that the debenture trustee shall appoint a nominee director on the Board of the company in the event of: (i) two consecutive defaults in payment of interest, (ii) default in creation of security for debentures, or (iii) default in redemption of debentures. **The Trap:** A single default does not trigger the nominee director appointment. Also, default in redemption of debentures is a separate trigger -- it is not the only trigger as the last option suggests. **Cross-Exam Context:** Debenture trustee obligations during default events are tested in NISM Series III-A and NISM Series IX exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The nominee director appointment is a protective mechanism for debenture holders -- it ensures oversight when the issuer shows signs of financial stress.
